column1: # environment: cpu=32 ram=64206MB disk=690708MB gpu=2xRTX_A5000 python=3.12.3 node=v22.22.0 network=disabled_by_policy
# goal: Reduce Brent fold1 h6-h8 mean MAPE to below 3% using AAForecast-based methodology on the fixed 8-week forecast setup.

# guard: UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest --no-cov tests/test_validate_only_aa_forecast.py tests/test_residual_config.py::test_load_app_config_marks_aaforecast_auto_requested_and_validated_modes tests/test_residual_config.py::test_package_exports_and_intentional_omissions_are_explicit tests/test_residual_config.py::test_model_filename_dict_includes_newly_supported_aliases tests/test_residual_config.py::test_supports_auto_mode_expands_to_newly_added_models tests/test_residual_config.py::test_build_model_supports_new_official_model_ports

column7: description
Fresh managed baseline from verify_brent_h6_h8_mape.py
Capture the AAForecast Brent baseline assets in git before the first optimization trial.
[labels: event_mask_sparsity] Use a robust upward-spike event mask for the first historical exogenous channel so AAForecast attention stays sparse instead of always-on.
Expand the Brent AAForecast exogenous pool with extra energy and stress proxies; the added context increased h6-h8 error instead of reducing the late-spike miss.
Revert the discarded feature-pool trial so the workspace returns to the retained sparse-event-mask baseline.
[labels: reactive_decomposition] Shorten the AAForecast seasonal/trend windows and lower the anomaly threshold so STAR-style decomposition reacts faster to short Brent spike regimes.
Add only Com_CrudeOil as extra Brent spike context; the paired oil signal still increased h6-h8 error relative to the retained reactive-decomposition run.
Revert the discarded single-oil-signal trial so the workspace returns to the retained reactive-decomposition baseline.
Increase encoder and decoder hidden size to 256 on the retained reactive setup; the extra capacity worsened Brent h6-h8 instead of helping the late-horizon spike.
Revert the discarded capacity-expansion trial so the workspace returns to the retained reactive-decomposition baseline.
[labels: lower_dropout] Remove encoder dropout on the retained reactive setup so the point forecast preserves more rare-spike signal instead of smoothing it away during training.
Broadcast the anomaly context across the full decoder sequence; pushing attended context to every timestep hurt Brent h6-h8 instead of helping the final spike horizon.
Revert the discarded attention-broadcast trial so the workspace returns to the retained reactive low-dropout baseline.
[labels: lower_anomaly_threshold] Lower the retained anomaly threshold from 2.5 to 2.0 so more pre-spike Brent timesteps enter the anomaly-aware path without changing the broader retained recipe.
Attempted to override training.input_size inside the Brent AAForecast case YAML, but app_config rejects duplicated shared setting paths such as training.input_size before a valid trial can start.
Reduce decoder depth to one layer on the retained recipe; the shallower head increased Brent h6-h8 error instead of reducing far-horizon smoothing.
Revert the discarded shallow-decoder trial so the workspace returns to the retained low-dropout, lower-threshold baseline.
Reduce encoder depth to one GRU layer on the retained recipe; the shallower encoder modestly regressed Brent h6-h8 instead of sharpening the final spike.
Revert the discarded shallow-encoder trial so the workspace returns to the retained low-dropout, lower-threshold baseline.
Reduce season_length from 4 to 2 on the retained recipe; overly short seasonal memory substantially worsened Brent h6-h8.
Revert the discarded short-season trial so the workspace returns to the retained low-dropout, lower-threshold baseline.
Move GPRD_ACT into the first event-channel position for AAForecast masking; the sharper geopolitical activity proxy destabilized Brent h6-h8 instead of helping the late spike.
Revert the discarded GPRD_ACT event-channel trial so the workspace returns to the retained OVX-led baseline.
[PIVOT] Micro-tuning decomposition memory, depth, and event-channel ordering did not beat the retained OVX-led recipe; switch to attention-selectivity tuning next.
[labels: narrower_attention] Reduce attention_hidden_size to 32 so AAForecast sparse attention scores critical steps through a tighter bottleneck and less diffuse weighting.
Reduce attention_hidden_size from 32 to 16; over-tightening the attention bottleneck worsened Brent h6-h8 instead of sharpening critical-step selection.
Revert the discarded attention_hidden_size=16 trial so the workspace returns to the retained attention_hidden_size=32 baseline.
[PIVOT] Attention bottleneck tuning found a retained winner at 32 but over-tightening regressed; switch next to multi-signal event-mask fusion across all historical exogenous channels.
Fuse all historical exogenous channels into the event mask; multi-signal event detection hurt the target Brent h6-h8 metric despite an apparently strong first-fold training phase.
Revert the discarded multi-signal event-mask trial so the workspace returns to the retained attention_hidden_size=32 baseline.
